reviews.review_msg: This class was not a positive experience overall, but that was mostly due to the crappy assignments and porrly written tests. John has excellent lecturing and presenting abilities. The time, however was wasted in explaining computer programs and not course material which could have been interesting

loved the class loved his method. Print out the notes before class, he lectures and you fill in more info to the notes. Didnt even need the text, altho helped with essays. Easy exams, simple grade breakdown, easy to pull up gpa!
Best TM ever, 1 day max turn around on papers. Friendly and helpful.
Great and funny prof, the T.A. was hilarious too, made dry subject interesting. Tough paper marker, but fair.

FANTASTIC! She is passionate, interesting, and gives fair exams. She is intelligent, and willing to help students through the boring text. She is encouraging and totally not one of the typical holier than thou profs/TAs you normally get at SFU.

Great intentions...but lack of english grammer skills made the class a little challenging. Also, his method of using one empirical study as a case study for the whole semster was difficult to understand until the end of the semester. Some of the concepts were pretty abstract (like conceptualization).
3 expensive text books, 2 of them can be considered communication bibles. lectures are a bit dry and irrelevant but overall, the prof is a humourous, nice guy.

he tries to be funny every time but the lecture material itself was quite confusing and you have to write down the notes every lecture cuz if you don't you won't really understand what the course is about.

He is really nice! Interesting lecture with a set of videos... However, if you dont grow up in the North American countries with watching the regular shows like Cops, Touched by a Angel, etc. you may find it is a little hard to understand the lecture content.
